## [00:00 - 03:00]

Most businesses are not designed with the exit strategy in mind. They're not set up to sell and they're not set up for long term success. Do you know that there's 31 and a half million small to mid-sized businesses in the United States? That's 31 million people with an entrepreneurial spirit. I've made all the mistakes that you can make pretty much as an entrepreneur and I know what it feels like to make those mistakes. These experiences have given me insight that I can help other business owners avoid so they can avoid those pitfalls and reap the rewards faster. You see of those 31 and a half million small to mid-sized businesses in the United States, about 25 million of those are single-employed businesses. The owner is the founder who's also the only doer. That's 25 million people who if they don't learn to grow and scale their business will never be able to sell, retire, or enjoy the hard-earned fruits of their entrepreneurial labor. Why? Because these businesses cannot function. In fact, it can't even exist without the owner managing the business's day-to-day responsibilities. They're setting a trap for themselves and they may never escape unless they devise and implement an exit strategy. Do you want investors? Do you want a buyer? You need to show proof of value. You do this by building a team, getting out of the way, and showing that the business isn't dependent on you. You see, most investors want to see in the clearest of terms, financials, marketing analytics, operational strategies. The business has a life of its own and it's not dependent on the founder of the business. Your passion should convert from the what you do to building a huge valuable organization that impacts the lives of the people that do it with you as well as the people you're doing it for. Your passion is creating a successful business. Theirs is investing in businesses that have a proven track record so that they can duplicate that success and make money on their money. That's what investors want to do. Otherwise, why would anybody want to invest or even purchase your business in the future? ## [09:01 - 11:12]

So here's the thing. If your mindset is, I don't plan to ever exit my business, well, I guarantee you're going to run a poorly run business. If your mindset is, I'm going to exit my business, then you're going to be focused on things that create value every single day in your business. So it really doesn't matter if your plan is to create, maintain a family business, a business you can retire through, a business that you can have longevity with your employees and create a great vehicle for your employees, success, it doesn't matter. What matters is, are you building a business that's maximizing its effectiveness, maximizing its profitability, maximizing its value every single day? Is that a mindset and a discipline you're going to have in your organization? Because if you run a business like that and you overperform, you can share the benefit of that overperformance, at least with your team, so you have high engaged, highly aligned team members who are excited to work for you. So here's the thing. The fact of the matter is this, you have to prepare your business for you not to be a part of it if you want life through your business. If you want your business to be your life, then captivate yourself in the business and not be able to extract yourself from it. The business owners I know are the ones that have a life through the business, they're not captive in the business. So if you run your business by maximizing value creation, it's disciplined, it has targets, it has big goals.
